title: 2006 BL8
summary:
2006 BL8 is a sub-kilometer asteroid, classified as near-Earth object of the Apollo group, that flew by Earth on 26 July 2013 at about 9 lunar distances. It is reported to be about 48 meters (157 ft) in diameter.
2006 BL8 was detected by the Catalina Sky Survey on 24 January 2006.
Some other NEOs noted for their Earth flybys in the summer of 2013, include 2009 FE (4 June 2013 at 9.6 LD), 2003 DZ15 (on 29 July 2013 passed at 7.6 LD), and  (277475) 2005 WK4 (on 9 August 2013 passed at 8.1 LD).
